meta_title: Impact Windows in North Lauderdale, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact windows in North Lauderdale, FL. 170mph wind-rated, code-compliant, insurance savings. Free quotes — $0 down financing.
h1: Impact Windows in North Lauderdale, FL — Built Tough for Broward County Storms
intro:
North Lauderdale may sit roughly eight miles from the Atlantic coastline, but anyone who has lived through a South Florida hurricane season knows that inland communities are far from immune to devastating wind and rain. Tucked between Tamarac, Margate, and Lauderdale Lakes in the heart of Broward County, North Lauderdale's zip code 33068 sits squarely within a 170-mph wind design zone — a designation that carries real consequences for every home, from the tree-lined streets of Ramblewood to the established neighborhoods of North Lauderdale Village. When a major storm takes aim at South Florida, the difference between a home protected by impact windows and one covered in plywood can mean the difference between minor inconvenience and catastrophic loss.

With an average home value of approximately $290,000, North Lauderdale homeowners have a significant asset worth protecting. Nearly half of the city's housing stock — an estimated 48 percent — was built before 1994, meaning thousands of residences were constructed before the sweeping post-Hurricane Andrew building code reforms that reshaped Florida's construction standards. Older single-pane aluminum windows common in these homes offer almost no meaningful wind resistance and provide zero impact protection against airborne debris, which travels like missiles in 100-plus-mph winds. Replacing these outdated windows with modern, laminated-glass impact windows is one of the smartest structural upgrades a North Lauderdale homeowner can make.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we understand the unique challenges facing homeowners in communities like North Lauderdale. Our team has completed more than 500 homes across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, and we bring 60-plus years of combined installation experience to every project. Whether you live near Hampton Pines Park or just a few blocks from North Lauderdale City Hall, we are your local experts for code-compliant, insurance-qualifying impact window installations. Backed by an A+ BBB rating and a reputation built on honest, in-house craftsmanship, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing — The Kings of Service — is ready to protect your most valuable investment.

section_why.h2: Why North Lauderdale Homeowners Trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
section_why.body:
Choosing an impact window company is not simply a matter of picking the lowest bid online. In a city like North Lauderdale, where older construction meets modern wind-load requirements and insurance companies scrutinize every detail of your wind mitigation report, the quality of your installer matters as much as the quality of your product. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing was built on a single, unwavering principle: every installation is performed by our own trained, in-house crew — never subcontracted. That distinction is not just a point of pride; it is a structural guarantee that the same experienced hands who quote your project are the ones who show up on installation day.

Our 60-plus years of combined team experience spans every home style found in Broward County, from the flat-roof CBS block homes common in North Lauderdale Village to the pitched-roof tile homes found throughout Ramblewood. We are intimately familiar with the permitting requirements of the City of North Lauderdale Building Division and know exactly what inspectors look for during final sign-off. We handle the entire permit process on your behalf — pulling permits, scheduling inspections, and ensuring your project closes cleanly with a Certificate of Completion you can hand directly to your insurance carrier.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's product lineup is curated specifically for South Florida's demanding climate. We exclusively install ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI impact windows — two of the most respected brands in the Florida market — because we refuse to put anything on a home that we would not put on our own. Every window we install is rated for the 170-mph wind zone that covers North Lauderdale, features laminated glass with Low-E coatings for energy efficiency, and is backed by manufacturer warranties that stand up over time.

Finally, we understand that home improvement projects require financial planning, particularly for North Lauderdale's lower-middle income households. That is why we offer flexible financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options available, making high-performance storm protection accessible to every homeowner in the community — not just those with large cash reserves. When you call Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, you are not just getting a contractor; you are gaining a long-term partner invested in the safety and value of your home.

section_history.h2: A City Shaped by Storms: North Lauderdale's Hurricane History
section_history.body:
North Lauderdale and the broader Broward County region have endured nine named storms since 1990, and each event has left lessons carved into the community's memory. The most formative of these was Hurricane Andrew in August 1992 — a catastrophic Category 5 storm that, while making landfall in Miami-Dade County, sent destructive wind fields well into Broward, exposing the alarming vulnerability of pre-code construction throughout the region. Andrew's legacy directly triggered the post-1994 Florida Building Code overhaul, but it also left a generation of North Lauderdale homeowners acutely aware of what inadequate window protection looks like from the inside of a house during a major hurricane.

Hurricane Wilma in October 2005 struck Broward County as a strong Category 3 storm and is widely considered one of the most damaging events in the county's recorded history. Wilma's winds tore through communities from Margate to Pompano Beach, and North Lauderdale was no exception. Thousands of homes lost windows and suffered interior flooding, roof damage, and mold infestations in the weeks that followed. The storm demonstrated that distance from the coast provides no meaningful buffer when a major hurricane crosses the peninsula. Then came Hurricane Irma in 2017 — a massive storm that maintained destructive winds well inland, knocking out power for weeks and causing widespread damage to homes still fitted with original single-pane aluminum windows.

This storm history is not ancient; it is recent, recurring, and statistically likely to continue. With nearly half of North Lauderdale's homes predating modern code requirements, the risk exposure for unprotected households remains significant. Impact windows are the most effective single upgrade available to reduce that risk — preventing breach, maintaining structural envelope integrity, and protecting families and belongings from the full fury of a South Florida storm.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Impact Windows in North Lauderdale
section_code.body:
North Lauderdale is located in Broward County and falls under the jurisdiction of the Florida Building Code (FBC) — specifically the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) adjacency provisions and the Broward County Local Amendments. While North Lauderdale is technically not classified as an HVHZ jurisdiction (unlike Miami-Dade and portions of Broward closer to the coast), the city still sits within a designated 170-mph ultimate design wind speed zone under ASCE 7-16 standards, which are incorporated into the current edition of the Florida Building Code. This means any impact window or exterior opening protection product installed in North Lauderdale must be tested, rated, and approved for performance at these wind pressures — and product approval documentation must be submitted with every permit application.

All impact window replacements in North Lauderdale require a permit pulled through the City of North Lauderdale Building Division. The permit package must include Florida Product Approval numbers (available through the Florida Department of Business and Professional Regulation product approval database), installation drawings prepared or approved by a Florida-licensed engineer or architect, and a Notice of Commencement for projects above certain thresholds. After installation, the project must pass a final inspection by a city building official who verifies that the windows are properly anchored, flashed, and sealed per the approved plans. Homeowners should never accept a contractor who offers to perform window replacements without pulling permits — unpermitted work can void insurance claims and create serious complications during home sales.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles every aspect of the permitting process as a standard part of our service. Our project coordinators prepare and submit complete permit packages, communicate with the City of North Lauderdale Building Division on your behalf, and schedule all required inspections. When the job is complete, you receive a fully closed permit — a documented, city-approved record that your home was upgraded to current code standards, which is exactly what your insurance company and future buyers want to see.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Windows: Premium Protection Engineered for North Lauderdale
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two industry-leading product lines in North Lauderdale: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI impact windows. Both brands are manufactured specifically for Florida's demanding wind and weather environment and carry Florida Product Approvals confirming compliance with the 170-mph wind zone requirements applicable to North Lauderdale homes. These are not entry-level products; they represent the same quality of window protection specified on new construction throughout South Florida's most discerning markets.

ESW impact windows feature heavy-duty multi-chamber extruded aluminum frames engineered to distribute wind load evenly across the frame system, reducing stress concentrations that lead to frame failure. Their laminated glass units — typically 5/16-inch to 7/16-inch total glass thickness depending on application — consist of two layers of tempered or annealed glass bonded to a high-strength PVB (polyvinyl butyral) interlayer. Even if the glass cracks under impact, the interlayer holds fragments in place, maintaining the weather barrier and preventing the catastrophic pressure equalization that causes roofs and interior walls to fail. Low-E (low emissivity) coatings on the ESW glass package also reflect solar heat gain, reducing cooling loads and lowering energy bills — a meaningful benefit in a city where air conditioning runs nearly year-round.

CWI impact windows bring comparable performance in a product line known for its versatility across different architectural styles, making them particularly well-suited for the diverse housing stock found in neighborhoods like Ramblewood and North Lauderdale Village. CWI's multi-chamber frame profiles offer excellent thermal performance, and their impact-rated units are available in a wide range of operating styles — single-hung, casement, fixed picture, sliding, and awning — so homeowners can achieve maximum storm protection without compromising the look and functionality of their existing window layout. Together, ESW and CWI give our North Lauderdale clients premium options at every price point, installed by our own expert crews to manufacturer specifications.

section_cost.h2: What Do Impact Windows Cost in North Lauderdale? Pricing, Financing, and ROI
section_cost.body:
The cost of impact window installation in North Lauderdale varies based on the number of openings, window sizes, operating styles, and whether any structural modifications are required. For a typical North Lauderdale home in the 1,400–1,800 square foot range — common in neighborhoods like Ramblewood — homeowners can generally expect to invest between $8,000 and $18,000 for a full-home impact window replacement, with most projects landing in the $10,000–$14,000 range. This figure includes product, labor, permitting, and all associated inspections. While that represents a meaningful investment, it is important to view it against the backdrop of what it protects: a home valued at approximately $290,000 and a household's irreplaceable belongings and safety.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing makes this investment accessible through flexible financing options designed specifically for homeowners across all income levels. With financing starting at just $87 per month and $0 down options available through our lending partners, there is no reason to delay storm protection waiting for a large lump sum. Many North Lauderdale homeowners find that the monthly financing payment is partially or fully offset by the immediate reduction in their homeowner's insurance premium — making the net out-of-pocket cost far more manageable than the sticker price suggests.

From a pure return-on-investment perspective, impact windows deliver on multiple fronts simultaneously. First, they qualify for Florida's sales tax exemption on impact-resistant products, reducing the upfront purchase cost. Second, they generate verifiable wind mitigation credits that lower insurance premiums — in many Broward County cases by hundreds of dollars annually. Third, they reduce energy costs by blocking solar heat gain and eliminating air infiltration around aging window frames. Finally, studies consistently show that impact windows increase home resale value, with buyers in hurricane-prone markets willing to pay a premium for a home they know is already fully protected and permitted. For a North Lauderdale homeowner, impact windows are not just a safety upgrade — they are a financial asset.

section_insurance.h2: Lower Your Homeowner's Insurance in North Lauderdale with Impact Windows
section_insurance.body:
One of the most compelling financial arguments for installing impact windows in North Lauderdale is the measurable reduction in homeowner's insurance premiums that follows a qualifying installation. Florida's insurance market — already among the most expensive in the nation — has grown significantly more costly for Broward County homeowners in recent years, with many households paying $3,000 to $6,000 or more annually for wind coverage alone. Impact windows directly address the single largest driver of those premium costs: wind and windborne debris damage, which accounts for the majority of catastrophic loss claims in South Florida.

The Florida Building Code requires insurance carriers to offer wind mitigation discounts to policyholders who can demonstrate that their home meets specific opening protection standards. A licensed wind mitigation inspector can verify your impact window installation and complete the OIR-B1-1802 Wind Mitigation Verification form — the document your insurance carrier needs to apply the discount. When all openings (windows and doors) are protected with rated impact products, homeowners typically qualify for the highest available opening protection credit, which can reduce the wind portion of their premium by 25 to 45 percent depending on the carrier and the age and construction type of the home. For a North Lauderdale household paying $4,500 per year in homeowner's insurance, that translates to $1,125 to $2,025 in annual savings.

Major carriers writing policies in Broward County — including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, and Heritage Insurance — all recognize and reward impact window upgrades through their wind mitigation discount programs. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides every completed client with a copy of their permit, Certificate of Completion, and all Florida Product Approval documentation needed to support the wind mitigation inspection process. We also encourage homeowners to schedule their wind mitigation inspection immediately after permit closeout so they can begin capturing insurance savings with the very next renewal cycle. Over a ten-year horizon, the cumulative insurance savings alone can recoup a substantial portion of the original installation investment.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact windows in North Lauderdale if I'm not right on the coast?
faqs.a: Absolutely. North Lauderdale sits eight miles inland but is located in a 170-mph design wind zone under the Florida Building Code. Hurricanes like Wilma in 2005 and Irma in 2017 caused widespread damage throughout inland Broward County communities. Distance from the coast does not reduce wind speeds to safe levels during a major hurricane — your home requires the same level of protection as any coastal property.

faqs.q: How much do impact windows cost in North Lauderdale, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Most full-home impact window installations in North Lauderdale range from $10,000 to $14,000 depending on home size, window count, and product selection. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down options available, so you can protect your home immediately without waiting to save up the full cost. Many homeowners find the monthly payment is offset by immediate insurance premium reductions.

faqs.q: What permits are required for impact window installation in North Lauderdale?
faqs.a: All impact window replacements in North Lauderdale require a permit through the City of North Lauderdale Building Division. The permit package must include Florida Product Approval documentation and installation drawings. A final inspection is required after installation. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire permit process on your behalf — from application to final inspection and Certificate of Completion — at no additional charge as part of our standard installation service.

faqs.q: How long does it take to install impact windows in a typical North Lauderdale home?
faqs.a: For most single-family homes in North Lauderdale, the actual installation typically takes one to two days for a full-home window replacement. Permitting and product lead times extend the overall project timeline to approximately four to eight weeks from signed contract to completed inspection, depending on current permit office processing times at the City of North Lauderdale Building Division and product availability. We keep clients updated throughout every phase of the process.

faqs.q: How much will impact windows reduce my homeowner's insurance in Broward County?
faqs.a: When all openings are protected with rated impact windows and doors, Broward County homeowners typically qualify for wind mitigation opening protection credits that reduce the wind portion of their insurance premium by 25 to 45 percent, depending on carrier and home construction. For a homeowner paying $4,500 annually, that can mean $1,100 to $2,000 per year in savings. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides all documentation needed for your wind mitigation inspection immediately after permit closeout.
